nodes.title:
Get Actor Rotation
La destinazione è Actor

Breakfall Event
La destinazione è ALS Base Character BP
On Landed: Temporarily increase the braking friction on lands to make landings more accurate, or trigger a breakfall roll.
nodes.comment: On Landed: Temporarily increase the braking friction on lands to make landings more accurate, or trigger a breakfall roll.

nodes.title: On Jumped: Set the new In Air Rotation to the velocity rotation if speed is greater than 100.
nodes.comment: On Jumped: Set the new In Air Rotation to the velocity rotation if speed is greater than 100.

nodes.title: Breakfall / Roll: Simply play a Root Motion Montage.
nodes.comment: Breakfall / Roll: Simply play a Root Motion Montage.

nodes.title: Mantle Timeline: This timeline is triggered from the MantleStart function and updates the function that lerps the character to the new location.
nodes.comment: Mantle Timeline: This timeline is triggered from the MantleStart function and updates the function that lerps the character to the new location.

On Movement Action Changed
La destinazione è ALS Base Character BP

IMPORTANT!!!

This blueprint contains a lot of simple logic meant to be built on or replaced by more sophisticated systems on a Per Project basis. How you manage Movement, Movement actions, Gameplay Events, and Character States should all be determined by the specific needs of your project. 

The most important thing is to make sure you are updating the correct Character Information in the interface to allow the AnimBP to function correctly.
nodes.comment:

This blueprint contains a lot of simple logic meant to be built on or replaced by more sophisticated systems on a Per Project basis. How you manage Movement, Movement actions, Gameplay Events, and Character States should all be determined by the specific needs of your project.

La destinazione è ALS Base Character BP

Mantle Update
La destinazione è ALS Base Character BP
These functions are a great place to perform logic based on the previous and new state, as they should be called whenever a state changes (as long as you use the Interface Event to change the state).
